title: Fix LLM parse failure signal — treat as evaluation signal not silent continue
description:
LlmEvaluatorImpl.ParseEvaluationResult catches all exceptions from JSON parsing and returns new EvaluationResult(false, "unparseable response") — meaning "no replan." Similarly, ExtractJson falls back to "{}" when no braces found, which also yields replan=false. A malformed LLM response silently suppresses replanning even when the current plan is actually failing.

Fix:
1. Add structured logging distinguishing failure modes: invalid JSON vs timeout vs low confidence vs truncation
2. Add response length caps and reject responses over a threshold
3. Log parse failures at Warning level (currently silent catch blocks)
4. Make ParseEvaluationResult and ExtractJson internal for testability
5. Add test suite: valid JSON, truncated JSON, prose-only, empty string, malformed braces, extra properties

Referenced in: Data/Pages/council/2026-06-30-external-audits-council.md
Audit source: Audit A
